title: plantago paralias subsp. affinis
intro: Plantago paralias subsp. affinis, also known as sea plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain family that grows in coastal areas and is characterized by its broad, flat leaves and small white flowers. Traditionally, sea plantain has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of sea plantain include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-allergic effects. Sea plantain can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in herbal remedies to enhance its medicinal properties.
